_note: Demo seed for Sumnohow CFO demo. 7 exposures, ~EUR 40M+ equivalent portfolio. Mix of statuses: HEDGED / IN PROGRESS / OPEN / BREACH. Update value_dates each year to stay live-looking.
_story: GBP/USD and CHF/USD onside (good hedges). EUR/USD in BREACH — hedged above current spot, showing the risk of incomplete policy compliance. EUR/NOK fully open — recommendation engine fires. GBP/NOK near-fully hedged, positive P&L story. JPY/USD 100% hedged. USD/NOK partially hedged, offside — margin call risk.
_refresh_dates: Update value_dates and end_dates annually — they should always be 2-6 months in the future.

exposures.description: UK commodity procurement — Q2/Q3 delivery, Liverpool port
exposures._story: 50% hedged — IN PROGRESS. Two tranches both below budget rate = positive locked P&L. Good story: disciplined hedging, room to add more.

tranches.notes: DNB Markets — locked before GBP strengthened. Ref DNB-GB-240301-01.
DNB Markets — topped up, best rate of the program. Ref DNB-GB-240318-02.

exposures.description: European supplier invoices — H1 2026, Amsterdam and Hamburg
exposures._story: 65% hedged — BREACH. Both hedge tranches placed when EUR was weaker; current spot (~1.088) has moved above the hedge rates AND above budget. MTM loss on hedged portion triggers BREACH status. Shows why partial hedging at wrong rate can still leave a company exposed.

tranches.notes: Nordea Treasury — EUR/USD fwd, hedged when EUR briefly dipped. Ref NOR-EU-240210-01.
Nordea Treasury — second tranche, rate slightly worse. Ref NOR-EU-240225-02.

exposures.description: EUR-denominated supplier invoices — Oslo HQ Q2/Q3 operational costs
exposures._story: 0% hedged — OPEN. EUR/NOK has drifted above budget rate, creating a floating loss on the unhedged portion. Recommendation engine will flag this prominently.

exposures.description: UK export receivables — North Sea contracts, multiple shipments Q2-Q4
exposures._story: 85% hedged — HEDGED. Three tranches across the program period, all above budget rate = strong positive locked P&L.

tranches.notes: SEB Corporate Banking — GBP/NOK first tranche, above budget. Ref SEB-GBN-240115-01.
SEB Corporate Banking — second tranche, still above budget. Ref SEB-GBN-240205-02.
DNB Markets — opportunistic hedge on GBP strength. Best rate in program. Ref DNB-GBN-240310-03.

exposures.description: Swiss precision equipment imports — single shipment Q3 2026
exposures._story: 50% hedged — IN PROGRESS. One executed tranche below budget = positive locked P&L.

tranches.notes: Nordea Treasury — CHF/USD forward, hedged below budget. Ref NOR-CHF-240320-01.

exposures.description: Japanese manufacturing components — rolling quarterly, Yokohama port
exposures._story: 100% hedged — HEDGED. Two tranches fully covering the exposure. JPY/USD below budget = meaningful positive P&L on a large notional.

tranches.notes: DNB Markets — JPY/USD first tranche, below budget. Ref DNB-JPY-240218-01.
DNB Markets — second tranche, even better rate. Fully covered. Ref DNB-JPY-240305-02.

exposures.description: USD-denominated import payments — North American grain, Houston port
exposures._story: 30% hedged — IN PROGRESS. CRITICAL: hedge rate (10.68) is above current USD/NOK spot (~10.50), making this forward OFFSIDE. MTM loss triggers margin call risk flag on Dashboard.

tranches.notes: SEB Corporate Banking — USD/NOK forward, hedged when USD was stronger. Now offside vs spot. Ref SEB-USD-240228-01.
